avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Slight increase in danger of moist avalanches in the course of the day.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Winter sport participants can release avalanches in isolated cases. These can reach large size. The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found in particular on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes above approximately 2000 m and on very steep west and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

As the day progresses the likelihood of moist slab avalanches being released will increase on very steep west and east facing slopes below approximately 2200 m. Caution is to be exercised in particular in areas where the snow cover is rather shallow.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack on west, north and east facing slopes, in particular above approximately 2000 m.

Towards its surface, the snowpack consists of faceted crystals, especially on shady slopes at elevated altitudes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be severely restricted. As a consequence of high relative humidity the snowpack can not consolidate, especially below approximately 2200 m.

In steep terrain there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface. This applies at high altitude.

tendency.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. Gradual increase in danger of wet avalanches in the course of the day.

avalancheActivity.comment: As the day progresses the likelihood of moist small and medium sized avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep slopes. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The wind slabs are in isolated cases prone to triggering. Avalanches can be released, mostly by large loads. In many places there is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

snowpackStructure.comment: The weather conditions have generally caused a reduction in the avalanche risk due to repeated cycles of melting and refreezing; nevertheless, the cooling is promoting a resumption of constructive metamorphism processes within the snowpack, leading to the formation of persistent weak layers that had been interrupted by the rise in temperatures. This process is occurring mainly where the snowpack is thinner. The weather conditions as the day progresses will give rise to moistening of the snowpack, in particular at low and intermediate altitudes. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weakly bonded old snow and wet snow require caution.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution.
Avalanches can be released in the old snowpack, even by a single winter sport participant. They can in isolated cases release deeper layers of the snowpack and reach quite a large size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular above approximately 2000 m and on steep northwest and northeast facing slopes. Caution is to be exercised in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was reduced. In the late morning the likelihood of slab avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep southeast and southwest facing slopes below approximately 2400 m. As a consequence of warming during the day individual mostly small wet loose snow avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Shady slopes: Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was reduced. The surface of the snowpack is hardly frozen at all and will already be soft in the early morning. This applies in particular below approximately 2400 m.

Some snow has fallen in some regions. The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations over a wide area.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding avalanches are the main danger.

snowpackStructure.comment: In gullies and bowls above approximately 1800 m a lot of snow is lying. The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack. These conditions will cause a gradual weakening of the snowpack also at intermediate and high altitudes. Further warming. Backcountry tours, off-piste skiing and ascents to alpine cabins should be started and concluded very early.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint. In some localities up to 5 cm of snow, but less in some localities, will fall above approximately 2000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of falling temperatures a crust formed on the surface. This applies in particular on sunny slopes. Restraint should be exercised because avalanches can sweep people along and give rise to falls. Weak layers in the lower part of the snowpack can be released even by individual winter sport participants in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example. This applies in particular on northeast, north and northwest facing slopes above the tree line. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack and reach medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment: The snowpack remains generally subject to considerable local variations. As a consequence of mild temperatures and solar radiation a crust formed on the surface. As a consequence of warming during the day moist avalanches are possible, but they will be mostly small. Distinct weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes above approximately 2000 m.

tendency.highlights: In some localities up to 5 cm of snow will fall above approximately 2000 m. The natural activity of small avalanches will only slowly increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: As a consequence of new snow a moderate danger of dry and wet avalanches will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Up to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Tuesday above approximately 1600 m.
In particular in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation and on very steep slopes small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized dry and wet avalanches are possible. They can in isolated cases be released, even by a single winter sport participant.

Restraint should be exercised because avalanches can sweep people along and give rise to falls.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 20 cm of snow, and up to 30 cm in some localities, fell on Tuesday above approximately 1600 m.
The new snow is lying on a crust and generally at low and intermediate altitudes. The new snow is lying on surface hoar in some places on shady slopes at high altitude.

High Alpine regions: Weak layers exist in the snowpack especially on shady slopes.

Over a wide area there is still a very large amount of snow.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate defensive route selection. Shady slopes where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially precarious.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2600 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released in some places by individual winter sport participants in particular above approximately 2400 m. This applies in particular in shady places that are protected from the wind. Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach large size in isolated cases.
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase on south facing slopes below approximately 2600 m.
Avalanches can occur in particular in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Towards its surface, the snowpack is hard and its surface has a crust that is strong in many cases.
Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.
In addition the fresh wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are easily triggered.
The surface of the snowpack has frozen to form a strong crust and will soften quickly. This applies in particular on sunny slopes below approximately 2600 m.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
As a consequence of warming moist small and medium sized avalanches are to be expected.
The wind slabs can be released, especially at their margins, in particular on steep shady slopes. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep sunny slopes below approximately 2600 m.
Dry avalanches can be released in deeper layers also.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The upper section of the snowpack is hard and its surface has a strong crust.
Precarious weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The backcountry touring conditions are mostly favourable.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some regions up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell on Tuesday above approximately 1800 m. The new snow is lying on top of a favourable old snowpack.

Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released especially by large additional loads. This applies especially on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m.

There is a danger of falling on the hard snow surface.

snowpackStructure.comment:
5 to 10 cm of snow fell on Tuesday above approximately 1600 m.
The old snowpack is well consolidated and its surface consists of loosely bonded snow lying on a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. The new snow is lying on surface hoar in some places on shady slopes at high altitude.
In particular in the vicinity of peaks snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Weak layers exist in the snowpack especially on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

avalancheActivity.comment: Above approximately 1400 m small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist and wet avalanches are possible.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Some snow will fall in the evening in some regions. The backcountry touring conditions remain generally favourable.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The snowpack remains generally stable.
The small amount of fresh snow that fell on Tuesday along the border with Piedmont may, in isolated cases, cause avalanches on extremely steep slopes above approximately 2300 m.
In these regions individual small moist snow slides are possible as the day progresses, in the event of prolonged bright spells in particular on extremely steep sunny slopes.
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in isolated cases and mostly by large additional loads. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on extremely steep shady slopes above approximately 2400 m and at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Towards its surface, the snowpack is largely stable and its surface has a melt-freeze crust that is strong in many cases. In steep terrain there is a danger of falling here.
In some regions 2 to 5 cm of snow fell on Tuesday above approximately 2200 m.
Especially steep shady slopes high altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Snow that is very compacted on the surface and stabilised by the stormy winds at the end of February, or composed of faceted crystals and, locally, snow with weak cohesion and surface frost. Weak layers exist in the old snowpack here.
Adjacent to ridgelines and in pass areas as well as at high altitude snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
In some regions 2 to 5 cm of snow, and up to 10 cm in some localities, will fall in the evening above approximately 2200 m. This snow will be deposited on surface hoar in particular on shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: The weather will be sunny at times. Slight increase in danger of moist and wet avalanches as a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation. This applies on very steep sunny slopes below approximately 2700 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released in isolated cases.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size in isolated cases on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. They can be released, even by a single winter sport participant.
Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was reduced. In the late morning the likelihood of slab avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep southeast and southwest facing slopes below approximately 2400 m. As a consequence of warming during the day individual mostly small wet loose snow avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night was reduced. The surface of the snowpack is hardly frozen at all and will already be soft in the early morning. This applies in particular below approximately 2400 m.

Some snow has fallen in some regions. The snowpack will be subject to considerable local variations over a wide area.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will increase a little during the day.

avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of warming during the day only isolated wet avalanches are possible.

Only isolated dry avalanches are possible. They can be released, in particular by large loads, but they will be small in most cases. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found on steep shady slopes at high altitude.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Shady slopes: Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

Outgoing longwave radiation during the night will be severely restricted. As a consequence of high relative humidity the snowpack can not consolidate. Sunny slopes: The weather conditions gave rise to moistening of the snowpack.
